title: Report: Young peoples access to affordable and quality housing
content: A new report from the European Education and Culture Executive Agency sheds light on housing affordability challenges faced by young people across Europe.

title: Gas coordination Group confirms readiness to prepare for the upcoming winter season
content: In a meeting of the Gas Coordination Group held this morning, the Commission and EU countries discussed the EU’s security of gas supply in view of the ongoing disruption in the Middle East.

title: Borderless innovations: EU security event in Narva
content: On 5 and 6 March, CERIS co-organised a conference on „Borderless Border Management“ in Narva, Estonia, right on the Schengen external border with Russia. More than 200 attendees from 20 countries came together to discuss solutions that will directly impact the security of Europe and Estonia.

title: Changes to support for people displaced from Ukraine in Poland
content: The Polish government drafted an official act to phase out measures introduced in 2022 to support people displaced from Ukraine by the ongoing war. Following a transitional period, beneficiaries of temporary protection would face reduced access to some family benefits, healthcare, and accommodation.

title: To Get Together project: promoting integration through culture in Slovakia
content: “To Get Together”, led by the Slovak Academy of Sciences with partners, supported people displaced from Ukraine by strengthening resilience and integration through safeguarding living cultural heritage.

title: First technical dialogue of the European Commission with urban stakeholders on Sustainable Tourism
content: A first pilot to experiment a new format of dialogue at technical level will be organised on the theme of sustainable tourism on 22 April 2026 in Copenhagen.

title: Commission welcomes selection of Lille as seat for EU Customs Authority
content: The Commission welcomes today's agreement between the European Parliament and the Council selecting Lille as the seat of the EU Customs Authority (‘EUCA').

title: Global Gateway in Vietnam: Commissioner Síkela promotes European investment with new focus on transport
content: European Commissioner for International Partnerships, Jozef Síkela, wrapped up a visit to Hanoi, leading a delegation of European companies to boost EU investment in Vietnam.

title: Eurostat: EU imports of energy products decreased again in 2025
content: In 2025, the EU imported € 336.7 billion worth of energy products, amounting to 723.3 million tonnes. Compared with 2024, imports decreased both in value (-11.1%) and in net mass (-0.6%).

title: Ukraine Donor Platform holds 16th Steering Committee – Kyiv, Ukraine
content: The Ukraine Donor Platform held its 16th Steering Committee on 24-25 March 2026 in Kyiv, attended by senior Government of Ukraine representatives, Platform members, observers and international financial institutions. In the margins of the meeting, members also exchanged views with senior business...

title: Commission welcomes historic agreement to reform EU Customs Union
content: The Commission welcomes the agreement between the European Parliament and the Council, delivering a landmark reform of the EU Customs Union. The most ambitious reform of EU customs rules since 1968 introduces new measures for e-commerce and launches a modern, data-driven customs architecture.

title: Join the EUI Panel of Assessors for the European Urban Initiative - Innovative Actions Call 4
content: Are you interested in evaluating the next wave of urban innovation projects across Europe? Are you an expert in one of the call topics? Do you have experience in evaluating project proposals? Become one of the Call 4 Assessors!

title: Gain the skills European employers need with vocational education and training
content: Jobseekers in today’s fast-changing EU market can learn the skills they need to take up a wide range of in-demand roles through vocational education and training (VET).
snippet: Jobseekers in today’s fast-changing EU market can learn the skills they need to take up a wide range of in-demand roles through vocational education and training (VET).

title: SAFE approvals unlock defence funding for Czechia and France
content: Following the approval of the first and second wave of defence funding, the European Commission has cleared two national defence plans under the Security Action for Europe (SAFE) initiative, further strengthening the Union's security and readiness. The Commission submitted a proposal to the Council
snippet: Following the approval of the first and second wave of defence funding, the European Commission has cleared two national defence plans under the Security Action for Europe (SAFE) initiative, further s

title: €115 million Programme for agile and rapid defence innovation (AGILE)
content: On 25 March 2026, The European Commission is presenting a new €115 million funding tool, AGILE, to bring disruptive defence technology from the lab to the field at record speed.
snippet: On 25 March 2026, The European Commission is presenting a new €115 million funding tool, AGILE, to bring disruptive defence technology from the lab to the field at record speed.

title: EU citizens show growing interest in electric vehicles, survey reveals
content: The European Alternative Fuel Observatory (EAFO) has published the Consumer Monitor 2025, offering new insights into consumer attitudes towards battery electric vehicles (BEVs) across the EU-27.
snippet: The European Alternative Fuel Observatory (EAFO) has published the Consumer Monitor 2025, offering new insights into consumer attitudes towards battery electric vehicles (BEVs) across the EU-27.

title: Commission and EU rail sector open nominations for Women in Rail Award 2026
content: This year’s Award will highlight individuals, teams and organisations that are driving change and creating concrete opportunities for women in the railway sector.
snippet: This year’s Award will highlight individuals, teams and organisations that are driving change and creating concrete opportunities for women in the railway sector.

title: EU boosts space cooperation with Norway and Iceland with Secure Connectivity Agreement
content: On March 26 2026 the European Union has signed an Agreement with Norway and Iceland allowing both countries to join GOVSATCOM and the Secure Connectivity Programme, IRIS². The Agreement highlights the EU’s commitment to cooperation with those countries.
snippet: On March 26 2026 the European Union has signed an Agreement with Norway and Iceland allowing both countries to join GOVSATCOM and the Secure Connectivity Programme, IRIS². The Agreement highlights the

title: EU check reveals misleading sales practices online
content: The European Commission and consumer protection authorities have discovered that some online discounts during Black Friday and Cyber Monday sales were misleading, thanks to the latest sweep.
snippet: The European Commission and consumer protection authorities have discovered that some online discounts during Black Friday and Cyber Monday sales were misleading, thanks to the latest sweep.

title: Commission scales up its humanitarian response in Lebanon to address urgent needs
content: This week, the Commission scaled up its humanitarian response in Lebanon, delivering nearly 150 tonnes of life-saving supplies to support people displaced by the deteriorating situation in the country.
snippet: This week, the Commission scaled up its humanitarian response in Lebanon, delivering nearly 150 tonnes of life-saving supplies to support people displaced by the deteriorating situation in the country

title: World Bank study provides analysis and recommendations to enhancing agricultural risk management in the EU
content: A new World Bank study assesses the use and effectiveness of agricultural risk management tools across EU countries and provides recommendations to support future policy development.
snippet: A new World Bank study assesses the use and effectiveness of agricultural risk management tools across EU countries and provides recommendations to support future policy development.

title: Commission releases new guidelines to future-proof Natura 2000 sites against climate change
content: A new guidance document will help Member States adapt the network of protected natural sites to the growing pressures of climate change.
snippet: A new guidance document will help Member States adapt the network of protected natural sites to the growing pressures of climate change.

title: €34.6 billion in cohesion policy funds reallocated to address EU's strategic priorities
content: Member States have successfully reprogrammed €34.6 billion in their 2021-2027 cohesion policy funds towards the EU's most urgent strategic priorities. These include boosting competitiveness, enhancing defence and civil preparedness, promoting affordable and sustainable housing.
snippet: Member States have successfully reprogrammed €34.6 billion in their 2021-2027 cohesion policy funds towards the EU's most urgent strategic priorities. These include boosting competitiveness, enhancing

title: Stay green, shine bright: how the EU Ecolabel is reimagining the hospitality business
content: How can hotels and campsites attract eco-conscious travellers, cut utility bills, and generate new business opportunities? Click to find out!
snippet: How can hotels and campsites attract eco-conscious travellers, cut utility bills, and generate new business opportunities? Click to find out!

title: Commissioner Kos visits Montenegro to show support for the country's EU accession path
content: Tomorrow and on Friday, Commissioner for Enlargement, Marta Kos, will be in Montenegro to reaffirm the EU's continued support for the country's accession process. To this day, Montenegro has opened all accession negotiating chapters and already closed 14, out of which six were closed last year...

title: Modernising our approach to tackling the rising wildfire threat
content: The EU's new strategy on wildfire risk management, which covers prevention, preparedness, response and recovery.
snippet: The EU's new strategy on wildfire risk management, which covers prevention, preparedness, response and recovery.

title: Commission holds a high-level policy dialogue on the future of the cement industry in Europe
content: Commissioner Hoekstra convened industry leaders to chart the cement sector’s path to net-zero. Talks focused on boosting demand for low-carbon products, strengthening carbon pricing and accelerating CO₂ infrastructure key to delivering competitiveness, resilience and climate ambition.
snippet: Commissioner Hoekstra convened industry leaders to chart the cement sector’s path to net-zero. Talks focused on boosting demand for low-carbon products, strengthening carbon pricing and accelerating C

title: Member States and social partners discuss gender dimensions of job quality
content: The Policy Analysis Group of the EU Employment Committee (EMCO) organised on 24 and 25 March a tripartite meeting with social partners to discuss the gender dimensions of job quality.
snippet: The Policy Analysis Group of the EU Employment Committee (EMCO) organised on 24 and 25 March a tripartite meeting with social partners to discuss the gender dimensions of job quality.
